ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to fruit tart exists; A00GG (Pastries and cakes) is the closest defensible parent category anchor. Used closest_parent match type as per allowed values.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for fruit tart as a food product; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

main_ingredients: fresh mixed fruits (strawberries, kiwi, blueberries, mandarin)

serving.description: 1 slice (approximately 1/8 of a standard 20 cm tart)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of fruit tart is one slice weighing approximately 90 g, equivalent to one-eighth of a standard 20 cm round tart.

nutrient_source.title: Fruit Tart — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Fruit Tart (cr_id F11110014). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at 5.8 g/100 g based on typical butter-based shortcrust pastry composition consistent with HPB data; monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and cholesterol set to -1 as detailed fatty acid breakdown is not reported in the HPB source entry.

gi_source.title: Flan, pastry case with custard filling — GI value from University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to fruit tart
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry for fruit tart in the University of Sydney GI Database. The closest defensible analogous food is a custard-filled pastry flan (GI approximately 55, classified as low), which shares the same structural components: shortcrust pastry shell, custard filling, and moderate sugar content. GL per serving calculated as: GI 55 × (carbohydrate per serving 27.0 g) / 100 = 14.9. Carbohydrate per serving = 29.7 g/100 g × 90 g serving = 26.73 g, rounded to 27.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 125 mg/100 g combined with saturated fat approximately 5.8 g/100 g from butter-based pastry shell
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit fruit tart to a small portion (≤90 g) and avoid versions with additional sugar glazes or heavy custard, as the combination of borderline sodium and saturated fat contributes to arterial stiffness and elevated blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrates 29.7 g/100 g with only 1.2 g dietary fibre/100 g and sugar 8.9 g/100 g; GI approximately 55 yields a glycaemic load of approximately 14.9 per 90 g serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat fruit tart as an occasional indulgence only, strictly limiting portion to half a standard slice (≤45 g), as the refined pastry shell and custard filling create a moderate-to-high glycaemic load that causes rapid postprandial glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 5.8 g/100 g from butter in pastry shell, combined with refined carbohydrates and added sugar 8.9 g/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit fruit tart consumption due to its saturated fat content exceeding 5 g/100 g, which raises LDL cholesterol; choose smaller portions and prefer versions with more fresh fruit topping and thinner pastry to reduce saturated fat and caloric density.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 5.8 g/100 g exceeds the 5 g/100 g RA threshold; refined pastry and custard base are pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or strictly limit fruit tart as the butter-based pastry shell provides saturated fat levels that activate TNF-alpha inflammatory pathways, and the refined carbohydrate base worsens systemic joint inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A fruit tart is a classic Western pastry dessert consisting of a baked shortcrust or sweet pastry shell (pâte sucrée or pâte sablée) filled with a smooth custard cream (crème pâtissière) or whipped cream, and topped with an arrangement of fresh seasonal fruits such as strawberries, kiwi slices, blueberries, mandarin segments, and raspberries. The pastry shell is buttery and crisp, the custard filling is rich, smooth, and lightly sweet, and the fresh fruit topping provides brightness, acidity, and visual appeal. Fruit tarts are of French origin and are widely served in European-style bakeries, patisseries, and cafés globally, including Singapore. Per 100 g, a fruit tart provides approximately 235 kcal, 3.0 g protein, 11.6 g fat (of which approximately 5.8 g is saturated fat from butter), 29.7 g carbohydrates, 8.9 g sugar, 1.2 g dietary fibre, and 125 mg sodium. The glycaemic index is approximately 55 (low), yielding a glycaemic load of approximately 14.9 per standard 90 g serving. Clinically, fruit tart is a high concern food for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its refined carbohydrate content and low fibre, and a moderate concern for those with hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to its saturated fat and sodium content. It is vegetarian and halal-suitable but contains gluten, dairy, and eggs.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a fruit tart?
What is the GI of fruit tart?
Is fruit tart suitable for hypertension?
What is a fruit tart made of?
How much sugar is in a fruit tart?
What is the serving size of a fruit tart?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11110014) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from typical butter-based shortcrust pastry composition consistent with HPB data. GI estimated from closest analogous food (custard-filled pastry flan) in the University of Sydney GI Database. All values are internally consistent and plausible.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available in HPB source): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. Detailed fatty acid breakdown, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for fruit tart are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (F11110014) as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat value of 5.8 g/100 g is an estimate based on typical butter-based shortcrust pastry composition and is consistent with the HPB source context. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on custard-filled pastry flan from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct fruit tart GI entry exists in the database. The input record listed dietary_tags as plant_based; this has been corrected to vegetarian as the food contains eggs and dairy (custard filling). FoodEx2 code A00GG (Pastries and cakes) uses match type closest_parent as no fruit-tart-specific FoodEx2 code was identified; this is an allowed match type value. No SNOMED CT code was identified for fruit tart as a food product.
